SOXX.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $436.91 (+0.9%) vs history prev_close $431.77 (+2.1%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: SPY closed at $708.45 (-0.28%) and QQQ at $651.42 (-0.62%) Thursday as software stocks sold off sharply — per Yahoo Finance, IBM and ServiceNow earnings triggered sector-wide weakness — while oil surged on renewed US-Iran Hormuz tensions entering week 7. VIX ticked up 2.1% to 19.31, remaining below the caution threshold but signalling edginess ahead of a critical earnings week: AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT all report April 30, representing the $1.7T hyperscaler capex thesis's biggest validation test to date. Intel surged ~19% in after-hours after smashing Q1 expectations per AFP, a notable reversal for a stock that had been in structural CAUTION — if gains hold at the open, INTC will reach record highs not seen since August 2000. The US 10-year yield ticked up to 4.32%, remaining above the 4.20% threshold that would ease NIM pressure on Singapore banks; the Fed's next rate decision is May 7, with J.P. Morgan Global Research expecting a hold.
commodities_note: WTI rose 2.16% to $96.59 — note a data anomaly: fast_info shows a prior close of $94.55 (+2.2%) while history-derived data implies a prior close of $92.96 (+3.9%); using the fast_info figure as the headline with history used only for technicals. The Hormuz disruption is now in its 7th week, with the ceasefire window closing April 27; analysts per rolling digest maintain a $100–135/bbl risk trajectory if escalation persists beyond 4 months. Gold eased 0.52% to $4,695.60 — still elevated but off recent highs. DXY firmed marginally to 98.85 (+0.14%), consistent with the US rate premium persisting above the ECB. No attractive entry exists for GLD at current levels given the extended run from the 50-day average.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS. You MUST assign ACCUMULATE unless a specific ticker-level thesis deterioration exists (see ASSIGNMENT RULE).

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at ACCUMULATE. DBS trades 0.74% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 56.30, with RSI at a neutral 48 — the cleanest technical position in the Singapore cluster. The invalidation level is SGD 56.30 (the 50-day SMA); a close below that confirms rally failure. Nearest resistance is SGD 57.74 (1.8% above). The headline R:R of 2.6:1 uses the observed SGD 56.30 support and SGD 57.74 resistance — however, note a tight-stop caveat: the invalidation is only 0.7% below current price, meaning normal daily volatility could trigger it before the thesis is tested. Use SGD 53.05 (200-day SMA, 6.5% below) as the mental anchor for position sizing — the sizing R:R to structural support is 0.3:1, so size conservatively. DBS earnings are April 30 — a binary event within 6 days that adds risk to any entry ahead of the print; the NIM compression story and any forward guidance will be the key read. US10Y at 4.32% remains above the 4.20% reset threshold, so structural NIM pressure persists; revenue growth is -3.3% and analyst consensus expects -8.8% earnings growth, which is a meaningful drag on valuation despite a forward PE of 13.7x (a slight premium to the cluster median of 12.1x). Volume is below average — size accordingly. Bottom line: DBS offers a 5.4% yield at a reasonable valuation, but entering 6 days before earnings with a tight stop carries binary risk. If you enter, use a starter position and be prepared for the NIM update on April 30 to set the next direction. ACCUMULATE — starter position. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. OCBC sits 2.59% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 21.11 — slightly extended for a clean entry, with RSI at a neutral 51.7. The R:R from here is 1.6:1 (observed: SGD 21.11 invalidation, SGD 22.51 target), which falls short of the 2.0 threshold needed for ACCUMULATE. Immediate support is SGD 21.59 (less than 0.3% below), meaning the stock is perched just above near-term support — a break there opens a retest of the 50-day. OCBC is the only Singapore bank showing YoY profit growth, a structural edge worth noting, but US10Y at 4.32% above the 4.20% NIM reset threshold keeps the structural headwind intact. The 3.85% dividend yield and price-to-book of 1.62x are reasonable, but the PEG of 2.89 (with revenue growth of only 6.5%) suggests the multiple is not cheap on a growth-adjusted basis. Earnings are May 8 — 14 days out, so no binary risk this week. Bottom line: OCBC is the best-positioned Singapore bank but not yet at an entry price worth acting on. Wait for a pullback to the SGD 21.11–21.20 zone, ideally with above-average volume.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from ACCUMULATE to CAUTION — UOB broke below its declining 50-day SMA of SGD 36.35, closing at SGD 35.88 (-2.97%), confirming the pipeline's base_scorer CAUTION signal. The pipeline's R:R is 1.6:1 (invalidation SGD 35.23, target SGD 36.89), but the sizing R:R to structural support at SGD 34.47 is only 0.7:1 — unfavourable from a fresh-entry perspective. Note the tight-stop caveat: the invalidation at SGD 35.23 is only 1.8% below current price, and given normal intraday ranges, use SGD 34.47 as the mental stop for sizing. The next major downside reference is the 200-day SMA at SGD 35.10, now just 0.8% below current price — a close below that level would signal meaningful deterioration. Analyst consensus expects -6.7% earnings growth, and revenue growth is -1.8%, with a forward PE of 10x (17.4% discount to the cluster median of 12.1x); the discount is real but not compelling enough to overcome a deteriorating technical picture. The PEG of 1.0 looks attractive but is inconsistent with negative revenue growth — treat with caution. UOB earnings are May 7. Bottom line: UOB broke support today on above-average selling pressure. No entry until the stock reclaims the 50-day average at SGD 36.35 with volume.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Pipeline hard-blocked to CAUTION — NVDA is 8.1% above its 50-day SMA of $184.66, RSI at 64.6, with only 11 days above the SMA, none of which qualify for a sustained trend exception. The Oracle-SMCI news (per TheStreet, citing Bluefin Research) adds a wrinkle: an alleged cancellation of 300-400 GB300 NVL72 units raises questions about the durability of some AI infrastructure relationships, though this is attributed to unnamed sources through a research firm and should be treated as unverified. The positive offset is Intel's after-hours surge on an AI-chip deal reference to NVDA, per The Wall Street Journal — but that's an Intel story, not a direct NVDA revenue catalyst for today. Fundamental picture remains compelling: forward PE of 17.8x against 73.2% revenue growth and PEG of 0.72 makes NVDA objectively cheap relative to growth, and the Blackwell Ultra ramp continues. The R:R at current prices is 0.8:1 (invalidation $184.66, resistance $212.17) — unfavourable. Support at $177.41 is 11.1% below; that's the level where the thesis would come under structural pressure. Volume is below average. Bottom line: Nvidia is the right company in the right cycle, but it's not at a buyable price today. Wait for a pullback toward $184–190 before reconsidering.

AMD.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 28% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD has become untradeable from an entry standpoint — at $305.33, it sits 39.5% above its 50-day SMA of $218.89 with RSI at an extreme 83.6, triggering the hard extreme-extension block. The SMA50 is 28% below current price, so a pullback to that level would require a crash-level move; use the $224.64 support zone as the more relevant near-term downside reference, still 26% below. The Meta MI450 framework (per AMD IR, Feb 24, 2026) remains the thesis backbone — up to 6GW of Instinct deployments through 2030 — but it is a framework ceiling, not a firm purchase order, and revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmark performance. A new Ryzen 9950X3D gaming CPU launch (per Zacks) adds a consumer catalyst but is a secondary driver. The 1-month gain of 48.7% has compressed the R:R to 0.2:1 at current levels. Forward PE of 27.8x with 34.1% revenue growth and PEG of 0.9 remain attractive fundamentally, but price has run far ahead of near-term earnings visibility; trailing PE of 117x reflects speculative positioning. Earnings are May 6. Bottom line: AMD is one of the most interesting AI names for the medium term, but today's price is not an entry point — the whole thesis is priced in and then some. This is a hold-for-existing-holders story; a cash investor waits for a meaningful pullback below $250 before engaging.

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Intel's after-hours surge of ~19% (per AFP and WSJ) after smashing Q1 earnings expectations materially changes the technical and fundamental picture. The WSJ notes Intel may have found a credible lane in AI through its chip-development deal with NVIDIA, though the 18A foundry thesis (no confirmed external customers as of today) remains unproven. If after-hours gains hold, INTC could reach record highs not seen since August 2000. At Thursday's close of $66.78, Intel was already 32.6% above its 50-day SMA of $50.35 — after a further 19% gap up, the extension would approach 55%+, making this one of the most extended names on the watchlist and entirely unenterable for a cash investor. The hard block remains: R:R at current (or gapped-up) prices is 0.2:1 with a 24.6% downside to the 50-day and only 5% upside to the nearest resistance at $70.12. FCF yield is -1.34% (negative — thesis depends entirely on future earnings recovery), forward PE is 59.7x (134.1% premium to the semis cluster median of 25.5x), and revenue growth is -4.1%. Earnings were today (April 24). Bottom line: Intel's Q1 beat is genuine news, but the stock is now priced for perfection after a 50%+ one-month run. Watch for a post-gap consolidation — no entry until the extension compresses toward the $58–62 zone at minimum.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from BUY to WATCH — the rr_distorted flag is true (headline R:R of 4.7:1 is produced by a tight 1.1% invalidation stop at $1,402.74, not a meaningful structural barrier), which blocks ACCUMULATE per gate 8. The sizing R:R using structural support at $1,314.33 is only 0.7:1 — unfavourable for a fresh entry. ASML sits 1.07% above its rising 50-day SMA of $1,402.74 with RSI at a neutral 50.6 — the cleanest technical position in the semis cluster. Resistance at $1,491.51 (5.2% above) is the meaningful upside target, but the distorted stop means position sizing is tricky. The proposed MATCH Act (per Simply Wall St.) seeking to restrict ASML exports to China is a real legislative risk — if advanced DUV/EUV sales to China are curtailed, a meaningful revenue stream is at risk, though the bill has not passed. Wells Fargo lifted its price target by $100, calling the pullback 'overdone' (per Insider Monkey, April 23). ASML's monopoly on EUV lithography and the High-NA EUV ramp for Intel's 14A node in H2 2026 underpin the structural thesis. PEG of 2.21 is elevated but the 19.2% expected earnings growth partially justifies the 29.8x forward PE; FCF yield of 1.51% is thin. China is ramping AI chip output using older DUV tools (per Tech in Asia), which is a workaround that limits future ASML upgrade cycles. Bottom line: ASML is the right company to own in the semis cycle, but the China legislative risk and distorted stop math make today a watch day, not an entry day. Re-enter the BUY list if the stock holds above $1,402 with above-average volume on a retest.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Micron is 16.7% above its 50-day SMA of $412.97 with RSI at 66.2 — hard-blocked from entry on both extension and momentum grounds. The pipeline-assigned R:R is 0.3:1 (derived, not observed) with the nearest support zones at $363.75 and $357.52, both 25%+ below. The structural bull case is genuinely compelling: SemiAnalysis founder Dylan Patel argued on the Invest Like the Best podcast that 'DRAM will double or triple from here' due to inference-driven demand outpacing supply — a bullish external thesis, though from an analyst whose firms may have positioning. Forward PE of 4.8x against 196% revenue growth and a PEG of 0.28 looks extraordinary, but note the PEG appears anomalous given a forward PE of 4.8x implies near-100% earnings growth already embedded — verify the growth estimate. FCF yield of 0.53% is thin, suggesting cash generation has not yet caught up to the earnings ramp. The HBM thesis (inference-driven KV cache scaling, not training) remains intact. With a 68% YTD gain per the 24/7 Wall St. article, the stock is pricing in a lot of good news. Earnings are not imminent (next quarter). Bottom line: Micron has one of the strongest fundamental cases on the watchlist, but it's simply too extended to enter now. A cash investor waits for a consolidation back toward $420–440 before sizing up.

TSM.signal_rationale: Pipeline hard-blocked to CAUTION — TSMC is 7.5% above its 50-day SMA of $355.97 with only 12 days above the SMA and RSI at 61.8, disqualifying the sustained trend exception. The R:R at current prices is 0.2:1 (observed: 7.0% downside to SMA50, 1.7% upside to $389.11 resistance) — deeply unfavourable. The structural thesis remains intact: CoWoS fully booked, scaling toward 120-130K wafers/month by end-2026, with NVDA booked for 50%+ of capacity and 5-10% price hikes on sub-5nm in 2026. The China AI chip capacity ramp using older DUV tools (Tech in Asia) adds a secondary demand signal. Taiwan geopolitical risk is priced in and doesn't change the signal on unchanged days. PEG of 1.25 and forward PE of 19.8x represent a 22.4% discount to the semis cluster median — genuinely attractive if the thesis holds. The next structural support is $330.99 (13.5% below), and the 200-day SMA is at $299.23 (21.8% below) — both far away. Bottom line: TSMC remains the AI supply-chain kingmaker, but it's not at a price where a cash investor should enter. Wait for a pullback to the $356–362 zone — the 50-day average — before reconsidering.

AMZN.entry_block: BLOCKED: +17.3% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=12 (<30), RSI 76 (>=55).

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Amazon is 17.3% above its 50-day SMA of $217.44 with RSI at a lofty 76.4 — hard-blocked from entry. The R:R is 0.1:1 (observed), with 14.8% downside to the SMA50 and only 1.4% upside to the $258.60 resistance. Earnings are April 30 — 6 days away — making this a binary event week. The Amazon-backed nuclear firm X-Energy raised $1.02 billion in a Bloomberg-reported IPO, reinforcing Amazon's energy infrastructure commitment for AI compute, but this is a secondary read-through rather than a direct near-term earnings catalyst. Jeff Bezos's Project Prometheus AI lab closed a $10 billion round at a $38 billion valuation per Bloomberg — an interesting ecosystem play but not an AMZN P&L driver today. The $200B AI capex guidance remains the central tension: when does this massive spend translate into AWS earnings acceleration? Forward PE of 26.9x with 13.6% revenue growth and PEG of 1.85 is neutral-to-slightly-expensive given peers. FCF yield of 0.87% is thin. Bottom line: Amazon is a high-conviction hold for those already in the name, but for a cash investor, there is no entry here at 17% above the 50-day with RSI near 76 and earnings in 6 days. Wait for the April 30 print to set the next level.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Pipeline hard-blocked to CAUTION — Google is 9.7% above its declining 50-day SMA of $307.87, with RSI at 67.3, and the SMA itself is not rising, compounding the extension concern. Tight-upside disclosure required: nearest resistance is $340.96, only 1.0% above current price — well within a normal daily trading range. The headline R:R uses a secondary target of $349.90 for a stated 3.6% upside, but this only materialises once $340.96 is broken; the true first-order R:R to $340.96 is 0.4:1 against an 8.8% downside to the 50-day SMA — unfavourable. Earnings are April 30, 6 days away — the biggest near-term catalyst. The TPU v7 story (Meta multi-year rental deal, Anthropic, Apple usage per Google Cloud Next) is the bull thesis, but TurboQuant algorithm displacement risk remains in its 29th day unvalidated — a latent tail risk. The Kyndryl expanded Google Cloud partnership (Simply Wall St.) signals enterprise AI adoption momentum. Forward PE of 25x and PEG of 2.31 make GOOG modestly expensive on a growth-adjusted basis relative to 18% revenue growth. SMA200 at $275.70 is 18.4% below — a meaningful drawdown from here would test that level. Bottom line: Google earnings April 30 is the one event that matters right now. The stock is not at a fresh-entry price today — wait for the print and re-evaluate at the resulting price level.

MSFT.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.6% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=8 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 60 (>=55).

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — Microsoft is 5.64% above its declining 50-day SMA of $393.56, triggering the hard block (>5% with a non-rising SMA and RSI at 59.8), and it dropped 2.73% today on reports, per Bloomberg, of buyouts offered to ~7% of its US workforce. The workforce reduction signals cost discipline — potentially earnings-positive — but the market read it as a demand signal concern. Earnings are April 30 — 6 days out. The OpenAI concentration risk remains the central analytical challenge: per the standing thesis, ~45% of MSFT's $625B commercial RPO is tied to OpenAI, and a portion of Azure AI revenue reflects circular credit consumption rather than arm's-length enterprise demand. The non-OpenAI RPO (+28%) is the cleaner signal to watch on April 30. Microsoft announced an A$25 billion (approximately $18 billion) Australia investment per Simply Wall St. — the largest committed investment in the country — adding to the capex buildout narrative. R:R is 0.3:1 (observed: 5.3% downside to SMA50, 1.7% upside to $422.72 resistance). Structural support at $381.71 is 8.2% below. Forward PE of 22x with PEG of 1.36 is the most reasonable valuation in BigTech given 16.7% revenue growth and 59.8% expected earnings growth. Bottom line: MSFT is approaching an attractive price zone, but entering ahead of a binary earnings event at 5.6% above a declining 50-day is not good risk management. The buyout news and OpenAI concentration story need to be assessed in the April 30 print before engaging.

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom at $419.94 is 23.7% above its 50-day SMA of $339.39 — extreme extension territory with RSI at 77.2, hard-blocked from any entry. The R:R is 0.3:1 (derived) with 19.2% downside to the SMA50 and only 5% upside to the $440.94 resistance. Motley Fool notes Broadcom hit record highs on AI partnership momentum; Zacks flags it alongside NVDA as one of only two stocks driving explosive 2026 earnings growth. The custom ASIC thesis — Google TPU, Meta training chips, OpenAI/ByteDance inference — is the central driver, with VMware adding recurring software revenue. This is a blended hardware+software model; do not benchmark valuation purely against semis peers. Forward PE of 23.2x with PEG of 0.92 and 29.5% revenue growth is genuinely attractive, and the AVGO-NVDA relationship is complementary (custom vs. merchant silicon) rather than competitive for most hyperscaler decisions. Earnings are June 4. Bottom line: Broadcom is doing everything right, but at 24% above its 50-day average with RSI near 77, there is no entry here. A cash investor waits for a pullback toward the $370–390 zone before engaging.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +16.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 55 (>=55).

LITE.signal_rationale: Pipeline hard-blocked to CAUTION — LITE is 16.2% above its 50-day SMA of $728.75 with RSI at 55 (at the threshold, disqualifying the sustained trend exception despite 202 days above the SMA). The R:R is 1.0:1 (observed: 13.9% downside to SMA50, 13.4% upside to $960 resistance) — borderline. The NVIDIA $2B strategic investment (confirmed per NVIDIA investor relations press release, March 2, 2026) for AI optics and US manufacturing buildout underpins the thesis. Lumentum's CEO Michael Hurlston reportedly told Bloomberg News on April 10 that the company is sold out through 2027 — a bullish call from a seller, which should be weighted accordingly. Simply Wall St. notes an analyst fair value upgrade from $655 to $773, reflecting AI optical demand and capacity news. Negative FCF yield (-0.04%) is a concern — the current revenue ramp has not yet generated meaningful free cash flow. Forward PE of 51.8x (103.1% premium to the semis cluster median of 25.5x) is a steep multiple that requires the sold-out-through-2027 story to actually materialise in earnings. Earnings are May 6. Bottom line: LITE has a genuinely differentiated NVIDIA-backed thesis, but it is too extended and too expensively priced to enter today. Wait for a pullback to the $800–810 support zone at minimum — ideally toward $728 (50-day SMA) — before reconsidering.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 76x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir dropped 6.22% Thursday, closing at $141.57 — below its declining 50-day SMA of $144.20 — and is now 8.5% below its one-month ago level. The pipeline soft-block flags the 76x forward PE as requiring explicit justification; the defense AI thesis (per the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling and $448M Navy ShipOS deal, per DoD press release) provides a rationale, but an IDIQ is a framework ceiling, not firm revenue, and the 647.6% expected earnings growth implied by analyst consensus is remarkable but needs multiple-quarter confirmation. The 193.4% PE premium to the BigTech cluster median is structurally unjustifiable without visible revenue acceleration. R:R is 0.6:1 (observed: 3.7% downside to $136.30 support, 2.3% upside to $144.86 resistance) — unfavourable. Structural support at $128.51 is 9.2% below — that's the next meaningful line if $136.30 gives way. Earnings are May 5 — 11 days out. Per BeInCrypto, an 'oversold setup with bullish divergences' argument is circulating, but RSI at 47.2 is not oversold by the contrarian gate definition (requires RSI <38). Bottom line: Palantir's defense AI story is real, but at 76x forward earnings with a below-SMA technical setup and unfavourable R:R, there is no entry here. Wait for earnings May 5 to reset expectations, then reassess if the stock holds above $136.30 with volume.

WRD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — WRD dropped 5.43% Thursday to $7.67, and despite being 5.56% above its rising 50-day SMA of $7.27 (triggering a hard block at this exact threshold), the decline brings it meaningfully closer to an actionable entry zone. The R:R is 1.6:1 (observed: 5.2% downside to the $7.27 SMA, 8.1% upside to $8.29 resistance) — not yet at the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE, and the hard block precludes that regardless. The WeRide thesis rests on commercial traction: 123% revenue growth, partnerships with Uber, Grab, and Bosch across 8 countries, and a pre-profit stage (~$98M revenue, RMB 7.1B cash). The ADR/China delisting risk is a standing invalidator that must always be mentioned — this is a high-speculative-risk vehicle. No recent news surfaced today. Earnings are May 21 — 27 days out. The SMA50 is rising, which is constructive for the intermediate trend. Bottom line: WeRide is approaching a level where an entry makes sense. A pullback to the $7.27–7.51 zone (50-day SMA to near-term support) with above-average volume would be the trigger. China delisting risk keeps position sizing small regardless.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster is split: DBS holds above its 50-day average (ACCUMULATE, earnings April 30), OCBC is slightly extended (WATCH), and UOB broke below its declining SMA today triggering a CAUTION downgrade. US10Y at 4.32% keeps the NIM reset threshold (4.20%) out of reach — day 46 of structural NIM compression. The divergence within the cluster is meaningful: OCBC's YoY profit growth makes it the relative standout, while UOB's breakdown today signals the weakest technical footing.
singapore.key_development: UOB broke its 50-day SMA today, separating the cluster into distinct tiers for the first time this cycle.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX rose 0.94% Thursday (data anomaly: history-derived move closer to 2.1%), but individual names tell a more cautious story — NVDA, TSM, MU, and LITE remain hard-blocked on extension, while ASML shifted from BUY to WATCH after the rr_distorted flag invalidated the headline R:R. The cleanest setup in the cluster is ASML (1.07% above SMA50, RSI 50.6), but even there the sizing math doesn't support ACCUMULATE today. Intel's after-hours Q1 beat is the session's headline catalyst — if gains hold, it will be deeply extended from a fresh-entry standpoint but validates the US foundry onshoring narrative that underpins ASML and TSM's structural thesis.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bullish structural thesis intact; near-term entries technically blocked across most names
semiconductors.key_development: Intel Q1 earnings beat sends shares ~19% higher after hours, potentially lifting the entire semis narrative but not creating actionable entries given extreme extension.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is in pre-earnings limbo — AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT all report April 30, and none offer attractive risk/reward for fresh entry today. MSFT's 2.73% drop on workforce buyout news (per Bloomberg) triggered a signal downgrade to CAUTION; PLTR's 6.22% decline keeps it firmly in CAUTION with a deteriorating technical picture. The cluster-level question for next week: do capex guidance numbers from the three hyperscalers validate or question the $1.7T AI buildout thesis?
big_tech.thesis_status: Pre-earnings binary — avoid new entries across the cluster
big_tech.key_development: MSFT drops 2.73% on workforce reduction news; all three hyperscalers report April 30 in the week's defining catalyst.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVIDIA strategic investment alongside LITE for AI datacenter co-packaged optics — directly linked to the LITE thesis and NVDA's photonics supply chain buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10-12% from recent highs, or revisit after LITE's earnings on May 6 for optical demand confirmation.

interconnected.name: TSMC (Taiwan listed, 2330.TW)
interconnected.reason: TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity directly constrains NVDA Blackwell Ultra shipments and AVGO custom ASIC production — the physical bottleneck for the entire AI buildout thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if US-listed TSM (ADR) pulls back to the $356–362 50-day SMA zone, which would suggest a more attractive entry level for the underlying Taiwan-listed shares on a relative basis.

interconnected.name: Super Micro Computer
interconnected.reason: Directly linked to NVDA GB300 NVL72 orders — the Oracle alleged cancellation of 300-400 units (per TheStreet, citing Bluefin Research) is an unverified but market-moving read-through for SMCI's near-term order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Only becomes interesting if the Oracle cancellation story is denied or retracts and SMCI holds above its 50-day SMA; avoid on unverified negative order news.

interconnected.name: X-Energy (nuclear energy)
interconnected.reason: Amazon-backed nuclear firm raised $1.02 billion in an IPO priced above the marketed range per Bloomberg, directly linked to AMZN's AI compute energy infrastructure strategy.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if shares pull back 15-20% from IPO price in the first 30 days of trading — post-IPO lockup dynamics and energy-sector positioning make early entry risky.

interconnected.name: Arm Holdings
interconnected.reason: Arm architecture underlies AMD's MI450 and NVDA Blackwell designs at the chip level — a sustained AI GPU buildout cycle directly drives Arm royalty revenue from every unit shipped.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes actionable if ARM pulls back to test its 50-day SMA with above-average volume, providing a technically clean entry into the AI silicon royalty stream.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz standoff enters week 7; ceasefire window closes April 27 with WTI at $96.59 and logistics rerouting ongoing.
US-China semiconductor decoupling: Section 122 tariffs active, China retaliating with 34% tariffs and export controls; MATCH Act bill would restrict ASML DUV/EUV exports to China.
Global logistics disruption: East-west shipping rerouting via Cape adds 10-14 days, compounding input cost inflation for semis supply chains.
US equity software sector sell-off: IBM and ServiceNow earnings triggered broad SaaS weakness; ServiceNow posted largest single-day drop since 2020.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz premium is showing up most directly in WTI at $96.59 — logistics cost reinflation is a secondary headwind for ASML and TSM supply chains and a primary pressure on Singapore banks' operating costs. NVDA remains hard-blocked partly because the extension was driven by AI optimism that now faces dual pressure from oil-cost inflation and the unverified Oracle/SMCI order cancellation story surfacing today.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Intel surged ~19% after hours on Q1 earnings beat (per AFP); Oracle allegedly cancelled 300-400 NVDA GB300 NVL72 units per TheStreet citing Bluefin Research (unverified); Microsoft offered buyouts to ~7% of US workforce per Bloomberg; Israel-Lebanon announced a 3-week ceasefire extension per Yahoo Finance (does not directly affect Hormuz).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, stay selective — the April 30 hyperscaler earnings triple-header (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT) is the week's defining event; no new positions in BigTech or extended semis until the capex guidance picture clears.

events_this_week.event: Intel Q1 earnings (after-hours April 23) — ~19% after-hours surge; if gains hold at open, INTC reaches record highs but becomes even more extended
Amazon (AMZN) Q1 earnings — $200B AI capex commitment and AWS revenue acceleration are the key metrics; earnings within 6 days adds binary risk to any entry
Alphabet (GOOG) Q1 earnings — TPU v7 revenue and TurboQuant algorithm displacement risk validation; primary BigTech signal catalyst
Microsoft (MSFT) Q1 earnings — non-OpenAI Azure RPO growth and Azure ex-OpenAI commentary are the metrics that cut through the credit circularity question
DBS (D05.SI) Q1 earnings — NIM trajectory and forward guidance will determine whether the ACCUMULATE signal is sustained or revised
Employment Cost Index Q1 — key Fed inflation input ahead of May 7 FOMC decision
JOLTS Report (March) — labor market health signal ahead of NFP
Palantir (PLTR) Q1 earnings — defense contract pipeline and government AI revenue are the thesis validators
FOMC Rate Decision — Fed expected on hold at 3.50-3.75%; any shift in forward guidance directly impacts Singapore bank NIM reset threshold (4.20% US10Y)
Non-Farm Payrolls (April) — after March's 178K recovery beat, April print validates or challenges labor market resilience narrative

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — pre-earnings caution with selective accumulation
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: April 30 hyperscaler earnings triple-header (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT) is the single biggest near-term risk; if capex guidance disappoints, extended semis and BigTech names face meaningful technical unwinds from elevated levels.

accumulate.note: Enter SGD 56.30–56.72 zone, invalidation at SGD 56.30 (50-day SMA) — use SGD 53.05 as the mental stop for sizing given tight nominal stop. Starter position — earnings binary risk April 30. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.
accumulate.entry_level: Enter SGD 56.30-56.72, mental stop at SGD 53.05

watch_for_entry.note: Cleanest setup in semis — 1.07% above 50-day SMA, RSI neutral. Actionable on a hold above $1,402.74 with above-average volume; stop below $1,314.
Best-positioned Singapore bank, but slightly extended. Actionable on pullback to SGD 21.11–21.20 (50-day SMA) with volume. Earnings May 8.
Approaching entry zone after 5.4% drop. Actionable at SGD 7.27–7.51 (50-day SMA to near-term support) with volume. Small position only given China delisting risk.

caution_trim.note: Avoid. 8.1% above 50-day SMA, hard-blocked; unverified Oracle order cancellation adds near-term uncertainty. No entry until pullback to $184–190.
Avoid. Hard-blocked at 7.5% above 50-day SMA with 0.2:1 R:R at current price. Wait for pullback to $356–362.
Avoid. Hard-blocked; declining 50-day SMA, earnings binary risk April 30, and TurboQuant displacement risk unresolved. Nearest resistance only $340.96 (1% away).
Avoid. Hard-blocked at 5.6% above declining 50-day SMA; workforce buyout news and OpenAI concentration risk need the April 30 earnings print to clarify direction.
Avoid. Hard-blocked at 16.2% above 50-day SMA; negative FCF yield and 103% PE premium to semis cluster median make this an expensive entry even with the NVDA photonics thesis.
Avoid. Below declining 50-day SMA, 76x forward PE, R:R 0.6:1. Defense AI thesis real but valuation unjustifiable at current levels. Reassess post-May 5 earnings.
Avoid. Broke below declining 50-day SMA today on 3% decline; 200-day SMA at $35.10 is the next support. No entry until reclaim of $36.35 with volume.

hold_no_action.note: Upgrades to WATCH if stock consolidates below $260 over 10+ sessions. No entry — 39.5% above 50-day SMA is extreme extension. Earnings May 6.
After-hours surge ~19% makes it even more untouchable for a cash investor. Reassess if stock settles and consolidates in the $65–72 zone post-gap. Earnings today.
Upgrades to WATCH if stock pulls back to $420–440 zone (near 50-day SMA). Structural HBM thesis intact — patience required.
No action until April 30 earnings. Reassess entry if stock pulls back to $220–230 zone post-print. RSI 76 and 17% extension from 50-day SMA rule out any entry today.
Upgrades to WATCH if stock pulls back to $370–390. Custom ASIC thesis intact — 23.7% above 50-day SMA with RSI 77 makes this a wait-and-watch situation. Earnings June 4.

new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10-12% from recent highs or after LITE earnings May 6

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: All three raise or maintain capex guidance, AWS and Google Cloud accelerate, MSFT non-OpenAI RPO grows >30% — validates the $1.7T AI buildout thesis
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → WATCH candidate if Blackwell demand signals appear in capex commentary
ASML → BUY re-entry if hyperscaler spend confirms equipment demand
AMZN → WATCH if RSI cools post-earnings and stock holds $240+ support
GOOG → WATCH if TurboQuant risk is dismissed and TPU revenue grows
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: One or more cuts capex guidance, Azure AI growth decelerates, or AWS margins disappoint — triggers sector-wide re-rating of AI infrastructure multiples
bearish_impact: NVDA → deeper CAUTION, $177 support becomes key level
ASML → risk of re-entering hard-block territory below $1,402 SMA
MU → HBM demand thesis questioned, $363 support tested
LITE → photonics demand narrative weakened, $800 support critical

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: NIM stabilisation signals or forward guidance shows rate sensitivity easing — upgrades Singapore cluster outlook
bullish_upgrades: D05_SI → ACCUMULATE conviction strengthened, consider adding to starter position
O39_SI → WATCH upgraded to potential ACCUMULATE if OCBC earnings follow suit May 8
U11_SI → CAUTION may soften to WATCH if sector NIM commentary is positive

bearish_impact: D05_SI → ACCUMULATE reverts to WATCH or CAUTION
O39_SI → WATCH stays WATCH with reduced conviction
U11_SI → CAUTION deepens; $34.47 structural support becomes the key level to watch

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Fed signals more confidence in disinflation, opens door to July cut — US10Y falls below 4.20% threshold
bullish_upgrades: D05_SI / O39_SI / U11_SI → Singapore bank cluster NIM reset threshold breached; upgrade all three to WATCH or above
MSFT → duration headwind eases; CAUTION may soften to WATCH if price has pulled back toward SMA50
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Fed signals no cuts in 2026, raises inflation forecasts — US10Y spikes above 4.50%
bearish_impact: Singapore cluster → U11_SI CAUTION deepens; D05_SI ACCUMULATE reverts to WATCH
GOOG / MSFT → forward PE compression on higher discount rate; both remain hard-blocked with deteriorating technical setups
